claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with:

**Do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'm asking about - Alex, who is 8 years old, and Blake, who is 9. They both live with me. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children, Alex (8 years old) and Blake (9 years old), who both live with you.

I have a few more questions to determine your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! And just to confirm a few more details:

**Are Alex and Blake in any of the following situations:**
- In hospital or residential accommodation for more than 12 weeks?
- In care (arranged by the local council) for more than 8 weeks?
- Living with someone else?

Or do they simply live with you full-time at home?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those situations apply. Alex and Blake both just live with me full-time at home. They're not in hospital, not in care, and they live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Just one more question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

(Remember, only one person can get Child Benefit for each child.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.**

Here's why:

**For Alex (8 years old):**
- Alex is under 16 years old
- You live with Alex and are responsible for them
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex

**For Blake (9 years old):**
- Blake is under 16 years old
- You live with Blake and are responsible for them
- You live in the UK
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake

You meet all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit. You can go ahead and make a claim for both children.

**Please note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility to claim - you can still receive the benefit and would just need to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex is not in hospital, care, or living with someone else. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, they are responsible for a child under 16, and they live with the child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 9 years old (under 16), lives with the claimant in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for Blake. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Blake is not in hospital, care, or living with someone else. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, they are responsible for a child under 16, and they live with the child. Therefore, the claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK with two children, Alex (8) and Blake (9), both under 16 years old. The claimant is responsible for both children, they live together full-time, neither child is in hospital or care, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for either child. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.

The claimant meets all the standard eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for both children.
